Scenario.overview: Energy Sector Simple This scenario outlines a targeted cyber attack against an energy sector entity, executed by an expert-level threat actor using a 'Simple' complexity approach. The attackers exploit supply chain vulnerabilities to gain initial access, leverage compromised user accounts for execution, and cause impact through data destruction.
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, User Account Authentication (Mechanism), File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers exploit a deserialization vulnerability in Oracle Agile Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) software, which is internet-facing, allowing them to compromise the system and gain unauthorized access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2021-27852 (Deserialization of Untrusted Data in Checkbox Survey)
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: Using credentials obtained from the initial compromise, the attackers authenticate as legitimate users to execute commands and install malware on the system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attackers modify files crucial to the operation of the energy sector entity, leading to data corruption and potential system failure, thereby disrupting operations and causing impact.

Scenario.summary: The C0010 campaign targets the energy sector by exploiting supply chain vulnerabilities to gain initial access, leveraging compromised user accounts for execution, and ultimately causing significant disruption through data destruction. The attackers demonstrate expertise in navigating complex systems and exploiting known vulnerabilities.
